cycle_label: GOV-05 — governance-thread standalone sprint · clean health-check sweep + GOV-04 F2/F3 route-out verification · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-05 = 5). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-05 opened 2026-05-14 from a fresh sweep. L01's headline: the sweep came back CLEAN. GOV-04's F2/F3 route-out to the telemetry-contract surface owner LANDED — F2 (telemetry-contract checker now scheduled, fires 23:47 nightly) and F3 (all 5 telemetry contracts passing) both resolved by the owner, neither re-surfaced as ownerless. Standing watches green, zero failed systemd units, no new ownerless work. GOV-05 is a light verification + codification sprint: L01 ran the sweep + verified the route-out; L02 codifies the durable finding (the GOV-NN fresh sweep is the verification gate that confirms a routed-out item was actually picked up by its owner) and closes.
goals: L01 — fresh health-check sweep + GOV-04 F2/F3 route-out verification. DONE. Sweep clean across all surfaces: 0 failed `systemctl --user` units, casey-pipeline + prod casey-junior healthy, rag indexers (darntech + dellatech) ran clean today, health-refresh-check green (all 6 pipelines ok). F2 route-out verified: observatory-telemetry-contract-check.timer is now enabled + active (fires 23:47 nightly) — the telemetry-contract surface owner scheduled it. F3 route-out verified: telemetry-contract-status.json (2026-05-14 21:33) shows 5 pass / 0 fail / 0 warn — the owner closed the failing contract. Neither re-surfaced as GOV-shaped: the route-out worked.

L02 — codify route-out-verification finding + close. PENDING. Durable finding to codify: GOV's route-out discipline (GOV-02 dellatech-chunking, GOV-03 NCAA-baseball, GOV-04 F2/F3) closes its loop at the *next* GOV sweep — the fresh sweep is the verification gate. A routed-out item that the owner resolves is the success case; one still ownerless re-surfaces as genuinely GOV-shaped. GOV-04's F2/F3 is the first full round-trip proof. Then close GOV-05 — clean sweep, nothing to carry.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-05 L01 is CLOSED — the sweep came back clean and GOV-04's F2/F3 route-out is verified landed. Remaining: L02 — codify the route-out-verification durable finding to governance memory (the GOV-NN fresh sweep is the verification gate that closes a route-out loop), then close GOV-05. GOV-05 carries nothing forward; GOV-06 scopes from a fresh sweep when next scheduled. Alternative if the operator wants GOV-05 to take on real work: the only dormant ownerless item is cycle-09 WS-B2 (Nextcloud content-sync auto-mirror substrate) — deferred in GOV-04 L02 with a context note, cycle-30+ discretionary, not critical. Default path: L02 codify + close.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-05 opened after GOV-04 closed clean. Scoped from a fresh health-check sweep per the operating model (scope from the sweep, never from a carryover list). L01 sweep ran same-session and came back clean — the headline finding is positive: GOV-04's F2/F3 route-out to the telemetry-contract surface owner landed and is verified.
stakes: The governance thread is cross-BU and standalone — nesting its sprint-logs inside one BU's foreman (darntech) stranded them when the active cycle moved, and made /brief unrunnable from the governance workspace. Its own foreman-enabled home makes the thread continuously briefable and parallel-runnable alongside whatever product cycle is active. GOV-05 closes the first full route-out round-trip: GOV-04 routed F2/F3 to the telemetry-contract surface owner with a paper trail, and the GOV-05 fresh sweep is the verification gate that confirms the owner picked them up — proving the route-out discipline is a closed loop, not a fire-and-forget.
notes: GOV-05 opened 2026-05-14 from a fresh sweep. The sweep is clean — this is a light verification + codification sprint, not a backlog-execution one. Primary finding (positive): GOV-04 L04 routed F2/F3 (telemetry-contract checker scheduling + failing contracts) out to the telemetry-contract surface owner with a paper trail; the GOV-05 sweep confirms the owner picked them up — F2 scheduled (timer enabled + active), F3 closed (5/5 contracts passing). First full round-trip proof that the route-out discipline closes its loop at the next GOV sweep. GOV-05 carries nothing forward (GOV-02 lesson: carryover backlogs rot) — durable findings live in memory files, not in carryover. GOV-06 opens from a fresh sweep when next scheduled. Governance-thread workspace remains a checkout of dacumen.git; Casey Junior deployment 09c22323.
